scenes.title: The Ice Cube Mystery
scenes.visualIntent: Close view of a labelled sealed bag on a plate; timer begins beside a sharp-edged ice cube, with a persistent WATER label.
scenes.checkpoint: What should we track: the cube shape, the water material, or both?

scenes.title: State, Shape and Material
scenes.visualIntent: A three-column board keeps WATER fixed while STATE changes from solid to liquid and SHAPE changes from cube to shallow pool.

scenes.title: A Fair Time-Lapse
scenes.visualIntent: Overhead setup diagram followed by a strip of evenly timed frames; safety icons show sealed bag, stable device and dry floor.
scenes.checkpoint: Why must the camera, plate and timing stay consistent?

scenes.title: Reading the Evidence
scenes.visualIntent: Three evidence frames with observation callouts separated from an inference panel; hidden droplets behind creases are acknowledged.

scenes.title: Conservation in a Closed Bag
scenes.visualIntent: A digital balance shows matching readings within a shaded precision band; a callout distinguishes conservation from perfect instruments.

scenes.title: Energy Enters the Story
scenes.visualIntent: Warm-room arrows point towards the colder sealed ice, while a crossed-out icon rejects flames, kettles and direct heaters.

scenes.title: A Simple Heating Curve
scenes.visualIntent: Large axes labelled time and temperature; three colour-coded regions read SOLID WARMS, MELTING, and LIQUID WARMS.
scenes.checkpoint: During which part are solid and liquid present together?

scenes.title: Why the Line Flattens
scenes.visualIntent: Energy tokens enter continuously; during the middle region they move a solid-to-liquid progress bar instead of lifting the thermometer.

scenes.title: The Particle Model
scenes.visualIntent: Side-by-side solid and liquid dot models retain identical dots; a MODEL, NOT PHOTOGRAPH banner lists what the drawing omits.

scenes.title: Freezing Reverses the Change
scenes.visualIntent: Reversible arrows connect liquid water and ice; energy arrows leave the tray towards the freezer coils, with a no-full-rigid-container warning.

scenes.title: The Cooling Curve
scenes.visualIntent: Cooling graph mirrors the heating graph, with animated state icons travelling across LIQUID COOLS, FREEZING, and SOLID COOLS.

scenes.title: Different Materials, Same Question
scenes.visualIntent: A comparison shelf shows ice, chocolate, butter and wax with different melting ranges; only sealed ice receives a CHILD OBSERVATION badge.
scenes.checkpoint: Does every material melt at the same temperature? Explain from the examples.

scenes.title: Melting Is a Physical Change
scenes.visualIntent: Sorting board contrasts reversible water state changes with app-only images of browning, burning and cooking; no hazardous action is demonstrated.

scenes.title: Challenge the Claims
scenes.visualIntent: Three misconception cards are stamped REVISE, then rebuilt into evidence-reasoning statements with matching visuals.

scenes.title: The Complete Melting Story
scenes.visualIntent: Final concept map links time-lapse evidence, conservation, energy transfer, curves, freezing and the model-limitation badge.
scenes.checkpoint: Explain melting in one sentence using material, state and energy.
